core_primitives.name: App
core_primitives.recommendation: Workflow / Chatflow for long-lived projects; legacy types only for simplicity

core_primitives.name: Graph Engine (graphon)
core_primitives.description: Unified DAG runtime under all app types; v1.9+ queue-based scheduling

core_primitives.name: Knowledge Base
core_primitives.description: RAG pipeline: ingest → chunk → embed → index → retrieve → rerank

decision_points.question: Conversation context needed?
decision_points.rule: if multi-turn memory required → Chatflow family else Workflow family

decision_points.question: Deterministic logic or LLM autonomous decisions?
decision_points.rule: hybrid wins for most production cases; pure Agent only for simple dialogue

decision_points.question: Cloud or self-host?
decision_points.default: Cloud Pro ($59) for teams ≤3; self-host for compliance / heavy volume / air-gapped

decision_points.question: Dify built-in KB or external vector store?
decision_points.rule: single app / single dataset → built-in; multi-app share / extreme RAG / >100M chunks → external

decision_points.question: Workflow getting unwieldy — what to do?

dilemma_cases.title: Visual ceiling hit — Dify or LangGraph/code?
Self-host vs Cloud — when is ops cost worth it?
Dify Knowledge vs separate vector store?
Agent app vs Workflow+Agent Node vs explicit Workflow?
Visual builder feels constrained — is Dify still right?

anti_patterns: Mega-workflow with 30+ nodes (canvas lag, hard to debug)
Code node carrying 100+ lines of business logic
Long-term project built on legacy Chatbot/Agent app types
No DSL exported to git for version control
Workspace-shared model API keys causing cost-accounting issues
Skipping retrieval testing on knowledge base
Long agent streams (>120s) without timeout tuning
Beta upgrade without backup (v1.9 destroys beta KB pipelines)
Direct container port exposed to internet (no reverse proxy)
Agent app for tasks with predictable path
not_for: General workflow automation with mostly non-AI integrations
Extreme RAG quality (KG-RAG, deep parsing) — use RAGFlow
High-frequency real-time inference router — use vLLM + custom gateway
Heavy-state agent runtime with time-travel — use LangGraph

ecosystem_positioning.vs_Flowise: Dify = production SaaS; Flowise = minimal chatbot editor (1GB RAM)
ecosystem_positioning.vs_LangFlow: Dify = own framework, multi-tenant; LangFlow = LangChain-coupled, exports code
ecosystem_positioning.vs_Coze: Coze = ByteDance hosted only + Feishu/Lark; Dify = open source, self-host
ecosystem_positioning.vs_RAGFlow: Dify = platform with RAG; RAGFlow = specialized RAG engine with thin UI
ecosystem_positioning.vs_n8n: Dify = AI-native; n8n = general automation with 400+ integrations and AI as plugin
ecosystem_positioning.vs_LangGraph: Dify = visual platform; LangGraph = code framework with rich state machine
ecosystem_positioning.vs_LlamaIndex: Dify = app platform; LlamaIndex = data/RAG framework. Often combined via External KB
ecosystem_positioning.vs_CrewAI: Dify = workflow + visual; CrewAI = role-based multi-agent in code. Often combined

surprising_findings: Dify's 'low-code' claim is contested — Chinese community openly argues that to build a real agent you still need Python (zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/1947389040702781389), making Dify's true value 'scaffolding compression' not 'no-code'

Pause-and-wait-for-user is a hard not-supported (issue #21455 closed 'not planned') — surprising for a 'production' platform claiming agent workflows. Forces hybrid LangGraph architecture for approval flows
Per-node DB queries cap throughput at ~10 QPS per pod regardless of model latency — the bottleneck is Dify's own engine, not the LLM (memo.d.foundation/breakdown/dify)
All Dify editions (Community/Premium/Enterprise) use the SAME Docker images — Enterprise features are unlocked purely by env-var flags pointing at LangGenius's API (discussions/32254)
The platform is honest about its limits in its own GitHub discussions — team transparently lists bottlenecks and roadmap, which is unusual for a vendor-backed open source project
